network.overall_strategy: This network is literature-constrained and deliberately centers the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic route to histidine: the Shen-Miller-Oró pathway from erythrose and formamidine to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, followed by Strecker conversion and hydrolysis to histidine. Auxiliary branches are included only as connected feeder, parallel imidazole, or evolutionary-context modules documented in the literature, not as independent validated histidine syntheses. Hydrothermal chemistry supplies reduced carbon feedstocks primarily as formate and formaldehyde precursors under Fe-S mineral catalysis; surface chemistry concentrates carbonyls, HCN, and sugars under borate, TiO2, and clay mineral control; biochemical/prebiotic settings complete hydrolysis, stabilization, and limited downstream assembly. The network therefore satisfies graph connectivity and convergence requirements while explicitly preserving the literature conclusion that histidine synthesis remains bottlenecked at formamidine availability and formation of imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formate

conditions.other: alkaline vent conditions; anoxic; H2-rich mixing zone

reactions.mechanism: Fe-S surface mediated reduction of CO2 by H2 to C1 reduced carbon as formate.
reactions.reasoning: Included as hydrothermal feeder chemistry rather than a histidine-specific step. Greigite-catalyzed CO2 reduction under alkaline hydrothermal conditions is supported as a plausible source of reduced C1 carbon, with highest yields near 75C and strong relevance to vent geochemistry (Lilova et al., 2020). This step is used conservatively to provide formate, not direct histidine precursors.

reactions.name: Surface conversion of reduced C1 carbon to formaldehyde reservoir

conditions.other: UV-exposed shallow pool; oxidant-free photoredox setting; step is speculative connector

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical disproportionation/reduction network converts reduced C1 species into reactive carbonyl inventory including formaldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: This connector is necessary to link hydrothermal reduced-carbon supply to literature-established surface sugar chemistry. The deep research identifies formaldehyde as a highly plausible prebiotic feedstock but does not specify a single exclusive terrestrial production route. TiO2-mediated surface photochemistry is consistent with prebiotic formamide and carbonyl photochemistry literature, though this precise conversion is a network-level speculative bridge and is marked as such (Saladino et al., 2012; Saladino et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Formaldehyde dimerization to glycolaldehyde

conditions.other: concentrating surface setting; carbonyl pool for formose-like chemistry

reactions.mechanism: Carbonyl coupling within formose-type chemistry generates the C2 aldose glycolaldehyde from formaldehyde-rich pools.
reactions.reasoning: Glycolaldehyde is identified in the research report as a highly plausible prebiotic sugar precursor and a direct precursor to erythrose. This step represents the standard carbonyl-coupling logic of formose chemistry in surface pools, with borate included to bias stabilization of sugars. The role of borate in sugar stabilization is supported by Ricardo et al. (2004), while the report identifies glycolaldehyde as a central prebiotic intermediate.

reactions.name: Aldol growth from glycolaldehyde to erythrose

conditions.other: pH 8-9; shallow evaporitic pool; borate stabilization of tetroses

reactions.mechanism: Aldol/formose extension of glycolaldehyde generates erythrose, with borate retarding degradation and stabilizing higher sugars.
reactions.reasoning: The research specifically states that erythrose can arise from glycolaldehyde self-condensation and related sugar chemistry and notes its instability. Yi et al. (2023) support erythrose formation near pH 8-9, and Ricardo et al. (2004) support borate stabilization of sugars. This is the key carbonyl feeder to the validated Shen route.

reactions.name: HCN and ammonia to formamidine reservoir

conditions.other: concentrating wet-dry surface pool; highly labile product; low plausibility acknowledged

reactions.mechanism: Nitrogen addition to HCN-derived one-carbon imidamide pool gives formamidine under concentrated aqueous conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Formamidine is explicitly identified in the literature as the critical but weakly plausible precursor required by the Shen histidine synthesis. This node is therefore necessary despite its low prebiotic confidence. The report states formamidine is conceptually derived from HCN + NH3 but is extremely labile and likely difficult to accumulate. This step is included with that caveat and should be read as a constrained feeder assumption rather than a robustly demonstrated prebiotic process (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Shen-Miller-Oro imidazole-4-acetaldehyde formation from erythrose and formamidine

conditions.catalyst: none demonstrated; minerals included as concentrating/stabilizing agents
conditions.other: aqueous solution; formamidine concentration 0.3 M in experiment; Schiff base formation followed by Amadori rearrangement; reported yield 1.6% from erythrose

reactions.mechanism: Erythrose condenses with formamidine to a Schiff base, followed by Amadori rearrangement and cyclization to the C4-functionalized imidazole aldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: This is the decisive literature-validated bottleneck step for prebiotic histidine. Shen et al. reported imidazole-4-acetaldehyde formation in 1.6% yield from D-erythrose and formamidine in aqueous solution, and the report emphasizes that this Amadori rearrangement is the sole demonstrated route to the crucial aldehyde intermediate. The same report also stresses its poor prebiotic plausibility because erythrose is unstable and formamidine is highly labile (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Strecker conversion of imidazole-4-acetaldehyde to histidine aminonitrile

conditions.other: classical Strecker setting; mildly basic aqueous conditions

reactions.mechanism: Aldehyde forms an imine with ammonia, cyanide adds to the iminium carbon, producing the alpha-aminonitrile precursor of histidine.
reactions.reasoning: The second conceptual stage of the Shen-Miller-Oró synthesis is a standard Strecker elaboration of imidazole-4-acetaldehyde with HCN and NH3 to the corresponding aminonitrile. This is directly stated in the deep research summary and is the only experimentally supported route from the imidazole aldehyde toward histidine (Shen et al., 1990).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of histidine aminonitrile to histidine

conditions.other: aqueous hydrolysis in prebiotic pool; anoxic; completes Strecker sequence

reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis via amide to carboxylic acid produces histidine.
reactions.reasoning: Hydrolysis of the Strecker aminonitrile completes the Shen histidine synthesis and yields racemic histidine. The reported overall histidine yield is about 3.5% relative to erythrose. This is the sole complete demonstrated prebiotic histidine synthesis in the literature (Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Debus-Radziszewski synthesis of imidazole from glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia

conditions.catalyst: none required in the original mild aqueous reaction; minerals included as plausible prebiotic concentrators
conditions.other: mild aqueous solution; acidic microenvironments can favor diimine intermediates

reactions.mechanism: Condensation of glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia via diimine intermediates gives imidazole ring formation.
reactions.reasoning: This reaction is included as a literature-supported parallel route to the imidazole heterocycle, not as a demonstrated route to histidine. Oró et al. demonstrated mild aqueous imidazole formation, and computational work by Kua et al. supported a 1,2-diimine intermediate. The deep report explicitly notes that such imidazole-forming chemistry does not solve the specific imidazole-4-acetaldehyde bottleneck for histidine (Oró et al., 1984; Kua et al., 2011).

reactions.name: HCN oligomerization to diaminomaleonitrile
reactions.mechanism: Self-addition of HCN generates the tetramer DAMN.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is a canonical product of HCN oligomerization and is identified in the report as a highly plausible prebiotic intermediate. This branch is included because it creates a connected HCN hub and leads to aminoimidazole chemistry, but not because it is a demonstrated histidine route (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970).

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN

conditions.other: strong UV irradiation around 350 nm on ozone-free early Earth

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical isomerization and ring closure convert DAMN to 5-aminoimidazole-4-carbonitrile.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report identifies near-quantitative UV conversion of DAMN to AICN as a robust prebiotic imidazole-forming route. This branch is retained for context and convergence on imidazole-rich chemistry, while explicitly not being treated as a direct histidine synthesis route (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of AICN to AICA
reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis converts the aminoimidazole nitrile to the corresponding carboxamide AICA.
reactions.reasoning: AICN hydrolysis to AICA is explicitly described in the report. AICA/AICAR provide an evolutionary and biochemical link between purine and histidine metabolism, but the report stresses that this does not supply a demonstrated prebiotic shortcut to histidine (Ferris & Kuder, 1970; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Formamide generation from HCN hydrolysis reservoir

conditions.catalyst: none essential; minerals serve as prebiotic surfaces

reactions.mechanism: Hydrolytic conversion of HCN-derived nitrile chemistry provides formamide-rich local reservoirs.
reactions.reasoning: Formamide chemistry is included because the deep report highlights it as an important surface feedstock for prebiotic heterocycle chemistry, though not as a source of histidine itself. This step is used only to connect the HCN hub to the Green-Xu-Sutherland branch and broader prebiotic chemistry, not to claim a validated histidine route (Saladino et al., 2012; Saladino et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Assembly of N-formyldehydroalanine nitrile precursor from glycolaldehyde and formamide-linked cyanide chemistry

conditions.other: cyanide-rich, mildly alkaline prebiotic surface setting; step abstracted as precursor assembly

reactions.mechanism: Cyanide/formamide/carbonyl chemistry generates a dehydroalanine nitrile framework suitable for downstream imidazole-forming cascade chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: The report identifies glycolaldehyde, HCN, and formamide as simple feedstocks underlying the Green-Xu-Sutherland 2023 imidazole cascade. This abstracted assembly step is included as a necessary literature-guided connector to the documented cascade product, but it is acknowledged as a network condensation rather than a stand-alone experimentally isolated step in the supplied summary (Green et al., 2023).

reactions.name: Green-Xu-Sutherland cyanide cascade to imidazole derivative

conditions.catalyst: cyanide-driven cascade under mildly basic conditions
conditions.other: pH 9.2; reported 63% yield of imidazole derivative

reactions.mechanism: Cyanide-triggered cascade forms three new C-N bonds and cyclizes to an imidazole derivative.
reactions.reasoning: This is a direct literature-supported modern route to prebiotic imidazole derivatives. The report emphasizes its high yield and promise, while also explicitly stating that extension to histidine has not been demonstrated. It is therefore included as a connected context branch, not a target-producing route (Green et al., 2023).

reactions.name: Histidine stabilization and mineral-assisted capture in prebiotic pools

conditions.other: adsorption/self-assembly on mineral-rich pool interfaces; stabilization against decomposition

reactions.mechanism: Histidine adsorbs, self-assembles, and is retained on mineral interfaces, improving persistence for downstream chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: This is not a synthetic transformation but a retention/stabilization node included to represent the biochemical/prebiotic fate of histidine once formed. The report notes histidine instability in aqueous solution and also documents self-assembly and mineral-assisted peptide bond formation on antigorite, pyrite, and aragonite surfaces, supporting the importance of post-synthetic capture (Madrigal-Trejo et al., 2021; Lazcano & Miller, 1996).

pathways.name: Canonical Shen route with hydrothermal C1 feed and borate-stabilized sugar branch
pathways.description: Hydrothermal reduced carbon is transferred to surface formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde, converted to erythrose under borate stabilization, then enters the sole demonstrated histidine route through formamidine, imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, Strecker cyanation, and hydrolysis.

pathways.name: Surface sugar-first Shen route
pathways.description: Assumes formaldehyde is already available in surface pools; emphasizes sugar growth and borate-stabilized erythrose prior to Shen condensation and Strecker completion.

pathways.name: HCN-rich concentrated-pool Shen route
pathways.description: Starts from surface HCN and ammonia accumulation to the weakly plausible formamidine reservoir, then converges with erythrose-derived chemistry to the validated histidine sequence.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-to-surface-to-biochemical full systems route
pathways.description: Uses the entire environmental progression from hydrothermal C1 reduction to surface sugar chemistry and final hydrolytic completion and biochemical capture of histidine.

pathways.name: Direct Shen completion from pre-existing erythrose reservoir
pathways.description: Represents the experimentally demonstrated core module most faithfully: erythrose and formamidine react to give imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, which undergoes Strecker conversion and hydrolysis to histidine.

pathways.name: Debus imidazole contextual branch converging on shared formaldehyde and ammonia hubs
pathways.description: A literature-supported imidazole-forming side branch from glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia. It does not directly make histidine but shares the formaldehyde/ammonia network that feeds the Shen route.

pathways.name: DAMN-AICN-AICA contextual HCN photochemistry branch
pathways.description: Surface UV photochemistry converts HCN to DAMN, then to AICN and AICA. This branch is evolutionary context for aminoimidazole chemistry and converges through the shared HCN pool rather than through a demonstrated histidine precursor.

pathways.name: Formamide-linked cyanide chemistry feeding Shen route
pathways.description: Uses the HCN-formamide reservoir branch as contextual surface chemistry while the actual target-forming sequence still proceeds through formamidine and the Shen bottleneck.

pathways.name: Glycolaldehyde-centered route with explicit erythrose convergence
pathways.description: Begins at the high-plausibility glycolaldehyde hub, then proceeds through erythrose to the validated Shen histidine sequence.

pathways.name: Validated histidine formation plus post-synthetic stabilization
pathways.description: Tracks the sole demonstrated synthesis to histidine and then its retention in prebiotic mineral-rich environments, reflecting the literature emphasis on instability and mineral-assisted capture.
